text: - (b) Subcontractor  will  adhere  to  Contractor's  Safety  Program  and  all  applicable  provisions  of  the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 and the regulations thereto (collectively, 'OSHA') and shall maintain working conditions which are free of unsanitary, hazardous, or dangerous conditions and shall take all reasonable precautions  for  protecting  the  health  and  safety  of  any  person  employed  in  the  performance  of  this  contract. Subcontractors performing  work  that requires the presence of a competent  person  (trenching/excavation, scaffolding, etc.), shall provide and maintain the appropriately trained competent person throughout the duration of the  work.  Subcontractor  shall  indemnify,  defend  and  hold  harmless  the  Contractor  and  Owner  from  all  claims, damages, fines, penalties, liability and/or loss, costs and expenses, including but limited to attorneys' fees, without limit, incurred in whole or in part, directly or indirectly, as a result of any breach of the obligations in this Section 9(a)  by  Subcontractor,  its  agents,  employees,  materialmen,  subcontractors,  or  any  other  party  for  whose  acts Subcontractor is liable. If any unsafe act or failure to adhere to OSHA or any other applicable law, code, regulation or  ordinance by Subcontractor, its agents, employees, materialmen, subcontractors, or any other party for whose acts  Subcontractor  is  liable  results  in  the  assessment  of  a  civil  penalty  against  Contractor,  Subcontractor  shall reimburse Contractor the amount paid therefor by Contractor on demand of Contractor and Contractor shall have the right to offset such amounts against payments then or thereafter due Subcontractor under this Subcontract or any other agreement.
